content: I've been looking for a good hosting solution for making all nightlies available and the easiest solution seems to be to setup a repo named nightly under roc-lang and put them all under releases there (separate tags). Github seems to be very flexible with the storage space for releases. Thoughts?
I think that's fine as long as we disable issues on it and have a README that explains the repo is just for storing ngihtly releases (to discourage PRs)
I tried to do that once, a few years ago, and it explicitly wasn't allowed...but maybe there's a way now?
